name: golang-github-subosito-gotenv-dev
title: golang-github-subosito-gotenv-dev
summary: Load environment variables from `.env` or `io.Reader` in Go.
description:
Load environment variables from `.env` or `io.Reader` in Go.
To modify your app environment variables, gotenv expose 2 main functions:
* gotenv.Load
* gotenv.Apply
By default, gotenv.Load will look for a file called .env in the current
working directory.
.
Behind the scene, it will then load .env file and export the valid
variables to the environment variables. Make sure you call the method
as soon as possible to ensure it loads all variables, say, put it on
init() function.
.
Once loaded you can use os.Getenv() to get the value of the variable.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-subosito-gotenv-dev because Load environment variables from `.env` or `io.Reader` in Go.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-subosito-gotenv-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-subosito-gotenv-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-subosito-gotenv-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
